published_prior_art.cite: Berge1957/Edmonds1965: routine finite matching enumeration, not a published causal exact-ID result; sources include https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.43.9.842 and https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/jres/69B/jresv69Bn1-2p125_A1b.pdf

repo_prior_art.cite: Closest repo items are pid_network_exposure_v1 finite joint-completion LP sharpness and pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair_v1 graph-count alias witness; neither is exact-ID matching-policy sufficiency.

published_prior_art.cite: Closest verified comparators: JohariLiLiskovichWeintraub2022 marketplace bias, AronowSamii2017 exposure mappings, Savje2024 misspecified exposure mappings, MasoeroVijaykumarRichardsonMcQueenRosenBurdickBajariImbens2026 multiple randomization; none states an alternating-path matching-sufficiency frontier.

repo_prior_art.cite: Closest repo items are pid_network_exposure_v1 and pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair_v1; neither gives an exact-ID alternating-path certificate for finite matching-summary alias diameter.

published_prior_art.cite: Berge1957 and standard symmetric-difference decomposition already supply the graph-theoretic path machinery; Chen2019 only covers simulable matching-market HT estimation, not summary loss.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship headline promises an alternating-path sufficiency frontier, but Exhibit 9.1 only computes the one-hop alias diameter for the four-node failure and no supplied SC10 field contains the required final structured verdict for the frontier object.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship headline promises a positive-alias-diameter certificate for arbitrary finite buyer summaries, but the only exhibit is the four-node example and does not compute the advertised certificate-extraction object on a worked generic instance.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship gap cites Savje2024 and MasoeroVijaykumarRichardsonMcQueenRosenBurdickBajariImbens2026 without a section, page, equation, or quoted open-problem anchor tied to the specific sufficiency-frontier claim.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The Chen2019 comparison is by broad paper-level claim only; the proposal gives no location-level anchor showing where simulable matching-market HT estimation leaves the summary-alias certificate gap open.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The radius-one class is defined using the informal phrase 'can affect b's matched status,' which is circular for a theorem whose conclusion is matching sufficiency.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: 'Path indicator differs' and 'deleting or adding that path changes theta_b' are not formally defined for an arbitrary finite buyer summary and arbitrary finite market class.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Exhibit 9.1 with separate worked exhibits for Conjecture 1 and Conjecture 2: compute the AP summary, the alias class, the certificate, and the focal probability change from Section 6 primitives, then include the full SC10 transcript with a final verdict line.
Make the radius-one class non-circular by defining it purely in graph-theoretic terms, then restate Conjecture 1 as a sufficiency theorem over that independently defined class.
Add location-level anchors for Savje2024, Chen2019, and Masoero et al. that identify the exact section, equation, or open problem that the matching-summary frontier is meant to tighten.

To reach the flagship floor, upgrade the result from a radius-one audit to either an iff frontier for a wider finite-market class or a polynomial-time identifiability algorithm with explicit input format, predicate, and complexity bound.

recommended_next_step: Reject this flagship attempt because the constructive focal objects are promissory; redraft only if the next version can exhibit the AP frontier and certificate extraction from primitives.
